Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

upanishads

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-12-01 · 9 answers
Hare Krishna,
one of my friend does not accept Upanishads. but accepts 4 Vedas..

how can I convince him that upanishads and vedas are meant to convince same message?
and upanishads are srutis


thank you haribol..
user [198] · 2009-12-01
Mayavadis dont accept the authority of Srimad Bhagavatam, Ramayana and other Puranas. According to them these are not bonafide scriptures. For them bonafide scriptures are only those on which Adi Sankaracharya has written commentary. Other scriptures are not bonafide.

If these people are adamant on not accepting upansidas and puranas there is nothing much you can do about it.

Hare Krsna.
user [38] · 2009-12-02
Puranas in Rigveda 9.99.4:

tauc0u7747 ghu257 thayu257 puru257 u7751 yu257 punu257 namabhyanu363 u7779 ata |
uto ku7771 pantadhu299 tayo devu257 nu257 u7747 nu257 ma bibhratu299 u7717 ||

http://www.sacred-texts.com/hin/rvsan/rv09099.htm

google finds 2 Atharvaveda refs:

Puru257 na site:http://sacred-texts.com/hin/av/
user [433] · 2009-12-02
What sampradaya doesnt accept the Upanishads? Ive never heard of such a thing, Sankaracarya did many Upanisad commentaries.

Do you mean to say your friend doesnt accept the Puranas?

I think your friend is very confused.
user [449] · 2009-12-02
he is not from any sampradaya. he is a disciple of fake guru.
user [154] · 2009-12-02
They were always accepted as shruti. THis is the reason for latter Upanisads to be highly sectarian: this was one of the strategies used by sectarian movements to legitimate their own texts through granting them the nominal status of sruti. This is the case with Sakta Upanisadas, such as Sita, devi, Tripura upanisadas. But principal upanisadas form part of the body of the text of the vedas and always cited "Sruti iti" by the commentators.
user [265] · 2009-12-03
[quote][cite] onkar:[/cite]Hare Krishna,
one of my friend does not accept Upanishads. but accepts 4 Vedas..

how can I convince him that upanishads and vedas are meant to convince same message?
and upanishads are srutis
[/quote]

No, the message of the 4 Vedas is mostly all about fruitive activities while the Upanishads are mostly all about liberation. If your friend has no desire for liberation, why should he bother reading the Upanishads?
user [38] · 2009-12-03
"Four Vedic samhitas only" is the idea of Arya Samaj.
user [449] · 2009-12-03
Haribol. VEDA prabhu is genious.
user [38] · 2009-12-03
